evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Model name 'claude-opus-4-8' appears fabricated (not a real Anthropic model identifier; current names are e.g. claude-opus-4-20250514, claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022). Also uses a top-level `cache_control={'type': 'ephemeral'}` kwarg on client.messages.create which is not a valid SDK parameter — cache_control is set inline on content blocks/tools, not as a request-level argument. These are core API misuses.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse, imports present, but two runtime breakers: (1) bogus model id will 404, (2) `cache_control=` kwarg on messages.create will raise TypeError/be rejected by API. Also `cache_control` placed on tool_use assistant block is not a valid placement. Multiple non-obvious fixes required.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: install bash, env var (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) listed and referenced, backend code in fenced blocks, models, test command (pytest). No frontend needed (FastAPI-only). Env var names consistent. Some duplicated streaming/non-streaming logic but acceptable.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites platform.claude.com/docs/...prompt-caching (close to canonical docs.anthropic.com) and an anthropics GitHub repo; rest are third-party blogs (requesty, markaicode, callsphere, langchain). Mix of one quasi-official + several third-party, scattered inline.

rubric_notes: Long-tail handling applied (docs_unavailable). Internal consistency is decent but model name 'claude-opus-4-8' is implausible and request-level cache_control kwarg is not part of the anthropic-python SDK signature — these are internal-inconsistency/hallucination signals lowering correctness.
